title: [Docs]: Add defence use-case blueprints without making the product defence-only
problem: nats-sinks is increasingly useful for mission-oriented environments, but the project must remain a general-purpose sink framework. Readers need concrete defence examples, while commercial, industrial, public-sector, and platform teams must still see that the product is not a single-use-case platform.
proposal: Create a dedicated documentation area for use-case blueprints. Defence should be one use-case family under that area, with pages for mission metadata, F2T2EA event phase tagging, sensor event custody, classification and labels, chain of custody, cross-domain handoff preparation, edge operation, and audit-oriented persistence. The pages must describe how to use generic nats-sinks features rather than requiring defence-only code paths.
users: External readers, solution architects, maintainers, mission-support integrators, security reviewers, and new users who need examples before mapping nats-sinks to their own environment.
delivery_semantics: Every blueprint must reinforce commit-then-acknowledge, at-least-once delivery, idempotent sink behavior, DLQ-before-ACK handling, and safe redelivery. The examples must not imply exactly-once delivery or autonomous operational decision-making.
security: The documentation must use sanitized example identifiers only and must not include live locations, credentials, private infrastructure details, sensitive operational procedures, or instructions for weapons-release automation. Defence examples should focus on event custody, persistence, auditability, metadata discipline, and controlled handoff.
acceptance: A docs use-cases area exists with defence as one sub-area.
F2T2EA event phase tagging is documented as metadata for audit, routing, persistence, and analysis.
Examples show Oracle JSON-column storage and file sink JSON output for mission metadata.
Documentation clearly states that nats-sinks is not a targeting, fire-control, or weapons-release system.
README.md and docs navigation link to the use-case blueprints without making defence the only project audience.
tests: Run markdown link checks, mkdocs builds, and documentation examples validation where config files are included. Add doc tests or snapshot checks for example JSON if a local pattern exists.
documentation: Add docs under a use-case folder, update README.md, docs/index.md, docs/oracle-sink.md, docs/file-sink.md, docs/security.md, docs/operations.md, ROADMAP.md, and CHANGELOG.md.
closeout: Close after the release with the use-case blueprint documentation is published. Include release tag, pages added, diagrams added, checks run, and follow-up blueprint ideas.
